message:
fix: use safeLazy for all route imports to prevent chunk load crashes (#3214) (#3215)

All 50+ lazy() route imports in App.tsx used an unsafe pattern that
crashes when stale chunks load after a deploy: the named export becomes
undefined and React receives { default: undefined }. This bypassed
ChunkErrorBoundary because the error message didn't match any known
chunk-load patterns.

Two changes:
1. Convert every lazy() call in App.tsx to safeLazy(), which validates
   the named export exists and throws a recognisable error on mismatch.
2. Add the "chunk may be stale" pattern from safeLazy to
   isChunkLoadMessage() so ChunkErrorBoundary catches it and triggers
   auto-reload recovery.

Co-authored-by: andy anderson <andy@andys-MacBook-Pro.local>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -63,6 +63,24 @@ describe('ChunkErrorBoundary Component', () => {
     expect(screen.getByText('Reload Page')).toBeTruthy()
   })
 
+  it('renders reload UI on safeLazy stale-export error', () => {
+    const ThrowError = () => {
+      throw new Error('Export "Compliance" not found in module — chunk may be stale. Reload the page to get the latest version.')
+    }
+
+    vi.spyOn(console, 'error').mockImplementation(() => {})
+    vi.spyOn(console, 'warn').mockImplementation(() => {})
+
+    render(
+      <ChunkErrorBoundary>
+        <ThrowError />
+      </ChunkErrorBoundary>
+    )
+
+    expect(screen.getByText('App Updated')).toBeTruthy()
+    expect(screen.getByText('Reload Page')).toBeTruthy()
+  })
+
